data.title: Forte Biosciences Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Forte Biosciences Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $2.45​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $-4.65. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of $3.27 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $-4.55 to $-1.76 per share.

  • Reported revenue was zero​; analysts expected zero.

  • Forte Biosciences Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $2.45​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $7.16 million.

  • Forte Biosciences Inc shares had fallen by 65.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 18.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the biotechnology & medical research peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Forte Biosciences Inc is $150.00

data.title: Lululemon Has Tough Road to Return to GrowthMarket Talk
data.description: Lululemon's guidance suggests there are no reasons to believe its growth rate will accelerate in a meaningful way, UBS analysts say in a research note. The stock of the yoga pants maker will also likely not go back to where it was at the beginning of the year either, the analysts say. Overall, it will take a lot to convince investors that Lululemon is again a big growth stock, the analysts add. UBS cuts its target price on the stock to $335 a share from $376 a share previously. Lululemon shares close down 14% to $293.06. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Musk Says xAI Acquires X in All-Stock Transaction Valued at $80B
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

xAI has acquired social media platform X in an all-stock transaction, Elon Musk said in an X post late Friday.

The deal values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion, including $12 billion in debt, said Musk, who is chief executive officer and founder of xAI.

"Since its founding two years ago, xAI has rapidly become one of the leading AI labs in the world, building models and data centers at unprecedented speed and scale," Musk said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: ChargePoint Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
ChargePoint Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of networked charging systems for electric vehicles (EVs), has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report highlights significant improvements in key financial metrics, strategic initiatives aimed at expanding market presence, and the challenges faced in the rapidly evolving EV industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $417.1 million, decreased from $506.6 million in the previous year, primarily due to a lower volume of Networked Charging Systems delivered.
  • Gross Profit: $100.7 million, increased from $30.1 million in the previous year, primarily due to the absence of the $70.0 million inventory impairment charges recorded in the prior year.
  • Loss from Operations: $(253.0) million, improved from $(450.0) million in the previous year, reflecting cost reductions from reorganization plans.
  • Net Loss: $(277.1) million, improved from $(457.6) million in the previous year, driven by increased gross profit and reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share - Basic: $(0.64), improved from $(1.22) in the previous year, reflecting the overall reduction in net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: ChargePoint generates revenue through the sale of networked charging systems, subscriptions to the ChargePoint Platform, and extended parts and labor warranties. The company also offers ChargePoint as a Service (CPaaS), which combines the use of ChargePoint's owned and operated systems with CMS and Assure programs into a single subscription.
  • Geographical Performance: ChargePoint has a strong presence in North America, holding approximately 61% market share in publicly available networked AC charging ports. Since launching operations in Europe in late 2017, ChargePoint has expanded to more than 20 European countries, aiming to establish a successful pan-European presence.
  • Sales Units: As of January 31, 2025, ChargePoint managed approximately 342,000 active charging ports in North America and Europe, including about 33,000 DC fast charging ports.
  • New Product Launches: ChargePoint continues to innovate with its comprehensive range of networked charging systems, including Level 2 AC charging stations and Level 3 DC fast chargers, designed for various applications such as commercial, fleet, and residential use.
  • Future Outlook: ChargePoint plans to maintain its leadership through efficient investment in the development of advanced networked charging system technologies and enhancing ChargePoint Platform solutions. The company aims to attract new customers while encouraging existing clients to expand their charging footprint as EV penetration grows.
  • Operational Strategy: ChargePoint focuses on three key verticals: commercial, fleet, and residential. The company uses a two-tiered, indirect fulfillment model to facilitate sales and grow its business internationally, relying on distributors and value-added resellers.
  • Manufacturing and Logistics: ChargePoint designs its products in-house and outsources production to contract manufacturers in the United States, Mexico, Asia, and Europe. The company deploys a global supply chain management team to ensure quality control and efficient delivery.
  • Government Regulation Impact: ChargePoint's operations are influenced by various government regulations, including privacy and data security laws, waste handling and disposal regulations, and incentives for EV infrastructure. Changes in these regulations could impact the company's business operations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Reorganization Plans: ChargePoint implemented several reorganization plans in September 2023, January 2024, and September 2024 to reduce operating expenses and increase efficiencies, resulting in workforce reductions and cost savings.
  • Market Expansion: The company focused on expanding its market share in North America and Europe by leveraging its networked charging systems and platform.
  • Capital Management: ChargePoint completed a private placement of $300 million in convertible notes, later amended to extend the maturity date to 2028 and increase interest rates. The company also entered into a $150 million revolving credit facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank, providing additional liquidity. During the fiscal year, ChargePoint raised $10.2 million through its ATM facility by selling common stock. The company has not engaged in equity repurchases or declared dividends.
  • Future Outlook: ChargePoint plans to continue optimizing its operations and supply chain to improve gross margins. The company anticipates using its cash on hand and cash generated from sales to meet working capital and capital requirements for at least the next twelve months. ChargePoint may seek additional equity or debt financing to support its growth initiatives and manage its capital structure effectively.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Operational Risks: ChargePoint operates in the early-stage market of electric vehicle (EV) adoption and has a history of losses and negative cash flows from operating activities, expecting to incur significant expenses and continuing losses for the near term. The company faces risks related to its ability to manage growth effectively in a dynamic and rapidly evolving industry. ChargePoint's success is highly dependent on the continuing adoption of EVs for passenger and fleet applications, and it currently faces intense competition, which is expected to increase as the market for EV charging develops.
  • Supply Chain and Cybersecurity Risks: ChargePoint relies on a third-party channel partner network of distributors and resellers to generate substantial revenue, and failure to expand this network may adversely impact its business. Supply chain disruptions, component shortages, increased tariffs, and manufacturing interruptions could adversely affect ChargePoint's ability to meet customer demand, leading to higher costs and adversely affecting its business and results of operations. The company is also highly reliant on its networked charging solution and information technology systems, which may be subject to cyber-attacks, service disruptions, or other security incidents.
  • Environmental and Regulatory Risks: ChargePoint's business is subject to risks associated with natural disasters and the adverse effects associated with climate change, including earthquakes, wildfires, or other types of natural disasters or resource shortages. The EV market currently benefits from the availability of rebates, tax credits, and other financial incentives from governments, utilities, and others to offset the purchase or operating costs of EVs and EV charging stations. The reduction, modification, or elimination of such benefits could cause reduced demand for EVs and EV charging stations, adversely affecting ChargePoint's financial results.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to improve financial and operational performance and execute its business strategy as critical to ChargePoint's success. The company is focused on expanding its sales and marketing capabilities to increase its customer base and achieve broader market acceptance of its solutions. ChargePoint is also working to manage its inventory effectively to avoid supply interruptions and excess inventory, which could adversely affect its financial condition and results of operations.
  • Market Risks: ChargePoint is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in demand for EVs and EV infrastructure, which could adversely affect its business. The company is also subject to risks related to global epidemics and health pandemics, which could have a material and adverse effect on its business and results of operations. Additionally, ChargePoint faces risks related to global supply chain constraints and changes in vehicle manufacturers' EV product goals, which may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and reduced demand for EV charging solutions.

SEC Filing: ChargePoint Holdings, Inc. [ CHPT ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Bluebird bio receives non-binding bid for up to $110.5 million
data.description:
Gene therapy maker bluebird bio said on Friday it had received a non-binding offer from peer Ayrmid for up to $110.5 million, sending its shares up 9.8% at $4.40 in extended trading.

Bluebird bio, which has been facing a cash crunch, first raised going-concern doubts three years ago. It laid off a quarter of its workforce last year to focus on launching three gene therapies, including Lyfgenia for sickle cell disease, which has had slow uptake.

Ayrmid, the parent company of cell therapy developer Gamida Cell, offered to acquire bluebird for $4.50 per share upfront, totalling about $43.8 million, according to Reuters calculations using data compiled by LSEG.

The privately held UK company also offered an additional $6.84 per share, representing about $66.6 million, contingent upon bluebird achieving certain sales milestones.

In February, bluebird bio said it had agreed to be taken private by Carlyle Group and SK Capital Partners, for $3 per share upfront along with the same $6.84 per share, contingent upon it achieving certain sales milestones.

Bluebird said its board is reviewing the Ayrmid proposal in consultation with its legal and financial advisors, and the board has not changed its recommendation in support of the Carlyle and SK Capital Partners deal.

data.title: Rupert Resources Reports Audited Financial Results for the Ten Months Ending December 31, 2024
data.description:
TORONTO, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Rupert Resources Ltd (“Rupert” or the “Company”) announces that it has published its audited financial results for the ten months ending December 31, 2024 and accompanying Management’s Discussion and Analysis for the same period.

Both of the above have been posted on the Company’s website www.rupertresources.com and on the Company’s profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

As per its press release dated December 10, 2024, Rupert has changed its fiscal year-end from end-February to end-December to better align the Company’s financial statement and continuous disclosure requirements with its industry peers.

Financial Highlights

During the 10 months ending December 31, 2024, the Company spent $25,405,497 (twelve months ended February 28, 2024 - 31,528,290) on general exploration costs and purchase of property, plant and equipment. As of December 31, 2024, Rupert held cash or cash equivalents of $44,744,545. The Company recorded a net loss and comprehensive loss for 10 months to December 31, 2024 of $(8,142,325) (12 months ended February 29, 2024 - $(5,782,240) and a net loss per share of $(0.05) (12 months ended February 29, 2024 - $(0.04)).

All references to currency in this press release are in Canadian dollars.

Graham Crew, Chief Executive Officer of Rupert Resources said, “Our strong cash position of over $44 million at year-end provides a solid foundation for advancing Ikkari through critical development milestones in 2025. With the recent fundraising and excellent shareholder support the Company is well positioned to deliver the ongoing Environmental Impact Assessment & Engineering Studies for the Ikkari project, as well as systematically exploring to unlock value across our strategic land package in the Central Lapland Greenstone Belt”

Operating Highlights

During the 10 months ended December 31, 2024 and up to the date of this document, Rupert’s operational activities have been focussed on the Rupert Lapland Project Area and Ikkari in particular.

Regional Exploration Program, including Ikkari

The regional exploration program at the Rupert Lapland Project Area is designed to identify and evaluate the mineral potential contained in Rupert’s land package in the Central Lapland Greenstone Belt.

Since July 2020, the Company has been engaged in a diamond drill program to further evaluate discoveries made within the Rupert Lapland Project Area, including Ikkari, as well as continuing to generate new targets through base of till (“BoT”) sampling, which continues across the Rupert Lapland Project Area and specifically over geophysical anomalies of interest.

Ikkari Project Drilling

The 2023/24 drill program was completed during the second calendar quarter of 2024, comprising some 43,000 metres (“m”) of drilling, with 24,000m allocated to drilling within the Ikkari project footprint.

During the 2024/25 drill season the focus of drilling has been on hydrogeological testwork and monitoring as well as further geotechnical investigations to facilitate the full optimisation of the project at the definitive feasibility study stage, which is expected to follow on from successful completion of the Ikkari PFS.

Continuing Exploration

Following on from the exploration campaign during the winter 2023/24, including the discovery of widths and tenors of mineralisation of interest at Heinä South, and the structural reinterpretation of the wider Area 1, the 2024/25 exploration program is now underway with drilling planned at six target areas along the 15km regional trend east of Ikkari as well as two base metals target areas located at the east of Rupert’s 100%-held property. The aim of the on-going season is to systematically explore extensions to the prospective structures identified in an updated structural interpretation.

Engineering and Ikkari Related Studies

The Company anticipates commissioning a definitive feasibility study later in 2025 and, in working towards this objective, has been and continues conducting metallurgical, geotechnical and hydrogeological field and study programmes.

Advancing Permitting and Environmental Work

Permitting, specifically progression of the environmental impact assessment (“EIA”) program and land use planning is also a key focus of the Company. The EIA Program was initially presented to the relevant environmental authorities in Finland on November 30, 2022 and formally started the environmental permitting process, with the aim of securing an environmental permit and thereafter a mining licence for Ikkari, in addition to those already held at Pahtavaara. The Company formally filed an EIA program with authorities during the second calendar quarter of 2023 and plans to file EIA report documents during the fourth quarter of 2025.

As part of this process the Company continues with numerous baseline environmental assessments, as well as on-going engagement across all stakeholder groups.

About Rupert Resources

Rupert Resources is a gold exploration and development company listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. The Company is focused on making and advancing discoveries of scale and quality with high margin and low environmental impact potential. The Company’s principal focus is Ikkari, a high-quality, multi-million ounce gold discovery in Northern Finland.

For further information, please contact:

Graham CrewChief Executive Officergcrew@rupertresources.comThomas CredlandHead of Corporate Developmenttcredland@rupertresources.com
 
Rupert Resources Ltd82 Richmond Street East, Suite 203, Toronto, Ontario M5C 1P1Web: http://rupertresources.com/

The words “may”, “would”, “could”, “will”, “intend”, “plan”, “anticipate”, “believe”, “estimate”, “expect” and similar expressions, as they relate to the Company, are intended to identify such forward-looking statements. data.title: Soy, corn bids flat in quiet trade
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn and soybeans were unchanged in the U.S. Midwest on Friday as farmers and buyers awaited a set of U.S. Department of Agriculture reports, which will be released on Monday.

* Corn and soy bids rose earlier in the week as buyers sought to entice selling from farmers who have been busy with spring planting preparations, dealers said.

* Grain markets were looking ahead to Monday's U.S. planting and stocks data from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, as well as seeking greater clarity on broad tariffs promised by U.S. President Donald Trump from April 2.

* Chicago Board of Trade corn futures on Friday slumped to the lowest price in more than three months on expectations of increased U.S. planting this year, before rebounding on a spate of fundamental trading and bargain-buying, market analysts said.

* Soybeans also fell for most of the session under pressured from a bumper Brazilian soybean crop, before ending higher on potential domestic demand after the Trump administration asked oil and biofuels producers to hash out a deal on the next phase of the nation's biofuels.

* The most-active Chicago Board of Trade corn contract settled up 3-1/4 cents at $4.53-1/4 a bushel, after earlier reaching its lowest since December 20 at $4.42 a bushel.

* CBOT soybeans ended 6-1/4 cents higher at $10.23 per bushel.

data.title: Regulatory Condition Satisfied for License Agreement between AbbVie and Gubra
data.description:
With reference to AbbVie's and Gubra A/S' (CPSE: GUBRA) joint announcement of March 3, 2025 regarding a license agreement to develop GUB014295, a potential best-in-class, long-acting amylin analog for the treatment of obesity, the US Federal Trade Commission today granted early termination of the waiting period under the HSR Act.

Closing of the license agreement remains subject to other customary closing conditions.

 

About the Phase 1 Clinical Trial of GUB014295

The Phase 1 clinical trial is a two-part, single center, double-blind (within cohorts), randomized, placebo-controlled, single (Part 1) and multiple (Part 2) ascending subcutaneous dose study of GUB014295. Part 1 has been completed; Part 2 is ongoing. More information on this trial can be found at https://www.clinicaltrials.gov/ (NCT: 06144684).

data.title: KoinBX Lists GALA Token Expanding Gaming and Entertainment Offerings
data.description:
Bangalore, India--(Newsfile Corp. - March 28, 2025) - KoinBX, a rapidly growing crypto exchange, recently listed the native Gala token of the Gala ecosystem - which includes music, games and entertainment in web3.

Image 1

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

With the growing interest in play-2-earn and Web3 entertainment projects, the listing of Gala aligns with KoinBX's mission of betterment for the project.

Saravanan Pandian, CEO and founder of KoinBX said, "Entertainment and gaming is undergoing a revolutionary change in the Web3 domain. As KoinBX continues to expand its GameFi offerings, we are thrilled to bring GALA to our global community." 

With strategic partnerships across the gaming and entertainment industries, including collaborations with gaming giants, Gala is at the forefront of the $200 billion global gaming industry's transition to Web3.
